The prophet’s spirit was willing, but the flesh was weak. We should ask God that whenever he speaks to us, he will awaken us, and then we should stir ourselves up. The church is like a golden lampstand set up to enlighten this dark world and to show forth the light of divine revelation. Two olive trees were seen, one on each side the lampstand, from which oil flowed into the bowl without stopping. God brings about his gracious purposes concerning his church without any skill or effort of humans; sometimes he uses people, but he does not need them. This represented the abundance of divine grace for making holy both the ministers and members of the church, and which no human power can obtain or prevent. The vision assures us that the good work of building the temple will be brought to a happy end. The difficulty is shown as a large mountain. But all difficulties will vanish, and all objections will be overcome. Faith will remove mountains and make them plains. Christ is our Zerubbabel; mountains of difficulty were in the way of his undertaking, but nothing is too hard for him. What comes from the grace of God may, in faith, be committed to the grace of God, because he will not abandon the work of his own hands.

The exact fulfillment of Scripture prophecies is convincing proof of their divine origin. Though the instruments are weak and unlikely, yet God often chooses such people to bring about great things through them. Do not despise the dawning light; it will shine more and more until the perfect day. Those who despaired of finishing the work will rejoice when they see Zerubbabel giving directions about what to do and making sure that the work is done. It is a comfort to us that the same all-wise, almighty Providence which governs the earth is particularly concerned about the church. All those who have the plumb line in their hands must look up to the Lord’s eyes, give constant regard to divine Providence, act depending on its guidance and submitting to its arrangements. Let us fix our faith on Christ and view him carrying on his work according to his own glorious plan and daily bringing his spiritual building nearer to completion.

Zechariah wanted to know what the two olive trees were. Zerubbabel and Joshua, this prince and this priest, were endowed with the gifts and graces of God’s Spirit. They lived at the same time, and both were instruments in the work and service of God. Christ's offices of King and Priest were foreshadowed by them. From the union of these two offices in his person, both God and man, the fullness of grace is received and given out. They built the temple, the church of God. So does Christ spiritually. Christ is not only the Messiah, the Anointed One himself, but he is the Good Olive to his church; and from his fullness we receive. And the Holy Spirit is the anointing which we have received. From Christ the Olive Tree, by the Spirit the Olive Branch, all the golden oil of grace flows to believers, which keeps their lamps burning. Let us seek, through the intercession and bounty of the Savior, supplies from that fullness which has hitherto sufficed for all his saints, according to their trials and employments. Let us wait on him in his ordinances, desiring to be made holy completely in body, soul, and spirit.
